phase3_findings.description: evaluate_eligibility's documented reason precedence contradicts the code: the docstring orders stale-override > no-digest-supplied, but no-digest-supplied returns from inside the overrides branch before the stale-override fallthrough. The suite pins the code's behavior by name (test_python_scripts.py:4130), so the docstring is the stale artifact.
Step 3.6 fallback classification depends on free-text judgment over unstructured stderr rather than a structured reason= tag (policy-without-mechanism).
main() has no top-level exception backstop for the documented 'queries always exit 0' contract; enforcement rests on per-call-site catches.
cmd_query_arm reuses the digest-unrecorded embed marker for the foreign-nonce cause; once persisted via record-dispatch --marker the two causes are indistinguishable in the rendered summary.
Four test gaps: _check_nonce mismatch path; load_state on-disk corruption via the CLI seam; route_arm prior_unreadable provenance at the query-arm seam; next_action compound budget+cap exhaustion.

Module docstring's 'Query subcommands ALWAYS exit 0' is true of answer-producing behavior but argparse exits 2 on a malformed invocation; a '(once correctly invoked)' parenthetical would disambiguate.
Two self-disclosed type-design trade-offs: non-verdict-on-arm TRANSITIONS rows are not runtime-load-bearing; arm/verdict tokens are bare strings rather than enums.

fix_decisions.claim_text: The lockstep assert's planted-defect positive control was actually run and observed RED, per the AC's requirement that this be recorded as evidence not attestation.
fix_decisions.evidence: The verifier searched only for assert_pin_red_under wrapping the lockstep in run.sh and concluded the control 'does not exist'. Issue #546's AC (line 69/120) requires the control be RECORDED IN THE PR — 'a table row removed via sed -E on a scratch copy, with the assert observed RED' — and reserves assert_pin_red_under for a DIFFERENT pin (line 121, the obey-the-tool behavioral-fix pin). The recording exists: PR #552 body line 29 ('drops module rows 33 -> 32 and the count assert is observed RED (672 passed / 3 failed vs 675 / 0 unmutated)') and the issue-546 workpad note at 00:36:03 carrying the exact sed -E expression and the observed tally. AC discharged; the verifier over-read the requirement.

fix_decisions.claim_text: record-creation-attestation's one-trailing-newline tolerance is documented and implemented consistently in issue-template.md's recipe (gh api ... --jq .body).
fix_decisions.evidence: The verifier confirmed the code's tolerance is real and correctly matches jq -r's raw-output framing (exactly one trailing newline), i.e. the underlying code is correct. It FAILed solely because the recipe lives in skills/create-issue/SKILL.md:296-300 rather than issue-template.md as the generator's claim asserted. That is a defect in the checklist claim's file attribution, not in the code — the engine's own verifier guidance directs PASS when the claim's wording is inaccurate but the code is correct.

fix_decisions.claim_text: Step 3.6 fallback classification is policy-without-mechanism (free-text stderr judgment).
fix_decisions.evidence: Over-grade shape 3 (uncorroborated single-source from silent-failure-hunter, an empirical over-grader; no other Phase-3 agent and no Phase-2 FAIL covers it) AND shape 1 (fail-closed): the agent's own blast-radius statement is 'does not itself lose data or corrupt state; the blast radius is workflow/UX, not correctness of the persisted audit record'. A misroute produces a loud degraded round or a refusal, never a silently wrong persisted verdict. Real and worth a follow-up; not an Important blocker.

fix_decisions.claim_text: main() has no top-level exception backstop for the 'queries always exit 0' contract.
fix_decisions.evidence: Over-grade shape 1 (fail-closed) + shape 3 (uncorroborated single-source from silent-failure-hunter). The agent states 'no live failure was demonstrated' and the risk is hypothetical-future ('if any FUTURE code path escapes the local catches'). The observable fail-direction is a loud non-zero exit with a traceback — a bounded stop, the safe direction — not a wrong value presented as an answer. Phase 2 VC-9/VC-10 confirmed every current StateError/_DigestError/OSError site is caught. Defence-in-depth worth doing; not an Important blocker on this diff.

The pin predates this fix and passes; the defect was the prose diverging from the pinned behavior, so no new pin is warranted (adding one would duplicate 4130).

shadow.trigger: early (engine_self_modifying, after iteration 1 — iteration 1 routed to fixes on a REJECT, so the convergence-time trigger had not run)
shadow.coverage_note: expected roster computed from the shadow's own Phase 0.5 (has_new_types=true -> type-design-analyzer; test-relevance predicate matches -> pr-test-analyzer). iterations:first-only was NOT applied (never applies to a shadow), so code-reviewer dispatched. 1:1 join: all 6 dispatched identifiers returned exactly one parsed, evidence-bearing result. No tripwire fired: the loop's last-iter phase3_dispatched carries both gated analyzers (no narrowing divergence), and checklist_skipped is null on both sides.

phase3_findings.description: The override eligibility ground has no precondition: cmd_record_override does not require any completed round (unlike record-revision/record-creation-epoch), and _valid_override is consulted before the no-verdict-round refusal. So init -> record-override -> query-eligibility answers eligible=yes ground=override with ZERO rounds dispatched and zero verdicts, and emit-body emits the never-audited body at exit 0 — defeating the module's stated remit that a caller ignoring the exit code cannot post an unaudited body.

A digest-unbound override fails open on a file-arm epoch: --draft-file is optional on record-override, and _valid_override skips the byte comparison entirely when draft_digest is None. A tampered, never-audited draft therefore answers eligible=yes and emit-body emits it. A guard whose comparand can be absent fails open exactly where it claims to fail closed.

The eligibility-token binding claim is false against HEAD: the changeset/docs/docstring say the token is bound to the answering digest or revision ordinal 'whichever ground answered', but on the override ground a digest answers while _yes is passed str(revision_ordinal(state)). Byte-distinct drafts mint an identical token — the replay the token exists to expose. Three coupled sites carry the claim.

The illegal-transition vs environmental-failure discriminator the SKILL.md routes on is unparseable free-form English; all nine _TRANSITION_REASONS tokens are dead vocabulary, never emitted. _load_for_mutation merges the two classes at one site (foreign nonce and corrupt state both surface as StateError -> exit 1).
phase3_findings.note: independently re-raises the same underlying concern iteration 1's silent-failure-hunter filed against SKILL.md:152-261 (different file+kind, so NEW under the Phase 3.2 signature rule) — a park-calibration signal that iteration 1's Suggestion grade was too lenient

phase3_findings.description: init --nonce <same> --force silently wipes a recorded creation attestation: cmd_init builds a fresh _new_doc whose creation is None, bypassing both forward-only guards; query-summary then renders attestation=none, which SKILL.md:300 defines as 'before any creation attempt' — indistinguishable from never-attempted.
evaluate_triggers docstring omits the third branch that unconditionally forces T2 true when the last completed round's outcome was 'no-verdict'.
_validate never asserts the outcome/pending mutual-exclusivity invariant that next_action relies on.
_validate does not re-check at load time the write-time rule that a user-decline override must name a surface.

shadow.over_grade_gate: no promote-path finding flagged: the two Criticals are fail-OPEN (shape 1 explicitly excludes a fail-open defect), the documented_falsehood is excluded from shape 2 by the truthfulness discriminator, and none is an uncorroborated single-source from silent-failure-hunter/pr-test-analyzer (shape 3) — all five promote-path findings come from code-reviewer.
